change: EVA+DoRA+rsLoRA LoraConfig, EVA calibration path, subgraph pool, per-stage save/resume, label mask fix

outcome: CRASH: Gemma 4 mm_token_type_ids IOB in multimodal scatter
CRASH: ValueError mm_token_type_ids required in train mode

claim: Gemma 4 decoder layers (transformers >=4.57) hard-require position_embeddings tuple (cos, sin), shared_kv_states dict, and per-layer-type rope dispatch. Wrappers that bypass Gemma4TextModel.forward must supply these manually.

outcome: CRASH: DTensor mixed with local Tensor on embedding

claim: FSDP2 fully_shard on base_model makes embedding.weight a DTensor. Custom wrappers calling get_input_embeddings()(input_ids) with local tensor input raise aten.embedding.default mixed-tensor error. Fix: register_fsdp_forward_method or explicit unshard.

config: removed root fully_shard, per-layer FSDP only
outcome: CRASH: stale EVA hooks from init_lora_weights='eva' fired on every forward, called gather_layer_inputs distributed op that crashed

claim: PEFT init_lora_weights='eva' installs activation-capture hooks on every LoRA Linear. If EVA calibration fails, hooks persist and crash on every subsequent forward. Drop EVA init entirely when FSDP+EVA bugs prevent calibration.

outcome: OOM 77 GB/rank: orphan per-layer shards didn't coordinate without root

claim: FSDP2 fully_shard is bottom-up. Applying fully_shard to decoder layers alone without applying it to the root module produces orphan FSDP units that don't coordinate gather/release. Each rank ends up holding full model replica.

outcome: SUCCESS first loss line: 2.78 → 1.82 over 5000 batches in 71 min

note: WITH LABELS BUG: labels=input_ids.clone() trained on full sequence including prompt (echo-mode)

claim: SFT label setup must mask prompt tokens with -100 before use as labels. Tokenize prompt separately to compute prompt_len, then labels[:, :prompt_len] = -100 AND labels[labels == pad_id] = -100. Without prompt mask, model learns echo-mode: reproduces prompt in output.

description: Run 9 completed Stage 2 but with echo-mode labels. Checkpoint exists in S3 but quality is compromised. Options: discard + retrain, or DPO on top to correct.

outcome: OOM 77 GB on cuda:0 during forward

claim: HuggingFace device_map='auto' for Gemma 4 31B uses sequential filling (cuda:0 first, spill to cuda:1), overloading cuda:0. 'balanced' skews similarly. 'balanced_low_0' puts everything on cuda:1. max_memory={i: 'XGiB'} forces even distribution.

outcome: OOM 78 GB on cuda:0 during first forward

description: Under device_map balanced, 50/50 param split confirmed (15.64B/15.91B) but cuda:0 fills to 78 GB during forward from non-activation source. Never isolated in session.

outcome: OOM 78 GB at DoRA weight + scaling * lora_weight

claim: PEFT DoRA materializes full [out, in] weight tensor at every forward via weight + scaling * lora_weight. For Gemma 4 31B all-linear target (60 layers × 7 modules/layer), intermediate weight tensors accumulate to ~50 GB per forward. Major memory cost unaccounted for in V4 spec estimates.

outcome: OOM 1.83 GB at HyperbolicMessagePassing einsum('rb,bio->rio', comps, bases)

claim: HyperbolicMessagePassing's einsum('rb,bio->rio', comps, bases) materializes a [num_relations, hidden, hidden] weight tensor. At num_rel=34, hidden=5376, bf16: 1.96 GB per MP layer. Factored form (sum_b comps[r, b] * (src_features @ bases[b])) avoids it, peak drops to tens of KB.

outcome: CRASH: CheckpointError recomputed metadata shape mismatch

claim: torch.utils.checkpoint(use_reentrant=False) does strict metadata validation on recomputed tensors. HyperbolicMessagePassing forward produces tensors whose save order differs between forward and recompute, triggering CheckpointError. Dropping the manual wrap works when factored _aggregate already removed the memory pressure.

outcome: Stage 1 completed in 13 min (vs 71 min with DoRA), Stage 2 first forward OOM 78 GB

note: Fast (use_dora=False made forward 5x faster than Run 6)

suggestion: Hypothesis: --stage all has different memory profile than --stage sft resume
outcome: rejected: Run 30 Stage 2 also OOM'd at 78 GB, same as --stage sft runs

claim: Iterating code fixes without instrumentation is wasteful. After 3 failures with same error signature (78 GB OOM, 442 MiB allocation), stop and add torch.cuda.memory_summary()/memory_snapshot() rather than guessing. This session burned 30 iterations without isolating the memory delta.

Pre-training verifier must exercise backward pass under the actual multi-GPU config. Forward-only verifier passes even when memory OOMs during backward, the labels bug exists, or gradient_checkpointing is miswired. Backward smoke test on minimum-viable inputs is essential.

HF's gradient_checkpointing_enable() configures decoder layers to self-checkpoint internally. Custom wrappers that call decoder_layer() directly still benefit — no need to manually wrap in torch.utils.checkpoint.checkpoint(). Manual wrapping on top double-wraps and can cause metadata-mismatch errors.

Stage 1 contrastive training does NOT train LoRA adapters — the base_model forward is inside torch.no_grad() for text encoding, so only gnn_projector receives gradients. LoRA in optimizer gets no grad. After Stage 1, LoRA weights are identical to init. Resume compatibility: LoRA state in after-contrastive is equivalent to fresh init.

rationale: Ship Run 9's echo-mode after-sft OR defer to spacetime graph rebuild. 30 iterations without training progress indicates architectural rethink needed, not more code patches.
